params.focusReason: liberalio focused to read her 202.5% proc per-hit (function-damage-rider crit test); boss Iron gives liberalio (Water) no advantage — element x1.0

method: ffmpeg contact sheets (overview 1/6s; FB-window zoom @30-35s fps=2 crop 760x420@960,180 x2). Read from stills.
notes: Function-rider crit test — INCONCLUSIVE (attribution corrected by scripts/probe/hit-values.ts). Colour convention (owner-confirmed 2026-07-14): orange + crit-icon = CRIT; red 'CORE HIT' = core; green = heal; damage pops at the CROSSHAIR, heals over the character. CORRECTION: the full hit-values table shows her 202.5% proc (base 1.13M-7.73M) and her 69% normal charge (base 1.13M-7.68M) OVERLAP almost entirely across the fight — so 1528543 CANNOT be pinned to the proc (it could be a low-buff normal). The orange 2038058 (=1528543 x1.333, a crit on an FB-raised major) is therefore a crit of SOME liberalio hit, but NOT provably her proc vs her (always-critting) normal. So this footage does NOT cleanly demonstrate function-rider crit. Red 'CORE HIT' hits (7405385, 2547572, 3017677) are normal-charge cores. To isolate the proc, need a window where proc and normal separate (they don't by value here) — or a different unit whose rider has a clean band (e.g. LM's DoT ~156-220k). ginmy dot_test remains the empirical basis. crit flags below = observed colour, NOT a hit-type attribution.

popups.note: orange = 1528543 x1.3333 (crit? or FB bonus? — see notes)
red CORE HIT — normal charge core
